paper_title: Cytological Analysis of Pollen Development in Ginger Species

methods.method_name: Cytological Analysis of Pollen Development Stages
methods.description: This method involves the cytological examination of anthers from four ginger species at different pollen development stages to observe morphological and histological events.

steps.description: Fresh flower buds of the four species at various developmental stages were collected from plants growing in natural habitats.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower buds at various developmental stages from natural habitats

steps.description: Anthers were carefully excised under a binocular microscope, measured, and classified according to their length and pollen development stage.
steps.searchable_summary: excise, measure, and classify anthers by length and development stage

steps.description: The four development stages, viz. microspore mother cell (MMC) stage, tetrad (TET) stage, uninucleate microspore stage and bicellular pollen (BCP) stage, were determined by examining squash preparations stained with acetic carmine under a Zeiss AX10 light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen development stages using squash preparations and acetic carmine staining

methods.description: This method involves preparing semi-thin sections of anthers for cytological examination to detect polysaccharides and lipids during pollen development.

steps.description: Anthers were fixed in 2.5% glutaraldehyde in 0.1 M KH2PO4 buffer (pH 7.2).
steps.searchable_summary: fix anthers in glutaraldehyde and phosphate buffer

steps.description: The samples were washed in buffer, post-fixed with 1% OsO4 overnight, dehydrated using an acetone series and embedded in Epon 812 resin, and then cured at 60℃.
steps.searchable_summary: post-fix, dehydrate, embed, and cure anther samples

steps.description: Semi-thin (1-2 μm) sections were cut with glass knives using a Leica Reichert Ultracut S ultramicrotome.

To detect polysaccharides by the periodic acid-Schiff (PAS) staining method, sections were oxidized for 10 min in 0.5% periodic acid in 0.3% nitric acid, rinsed in running water for 1-2 min with a final rinse in distilled water, stained for 60 min in Schiff's reagent, washed three times in 0.5% sodium metabisulfite for 2 min each, rinsed for 5 min in running water and then transferred to distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PAS staining to detect polysaccharides

steps.description: To detect lipids, sections were rinsed for 1-2 min in 70% ethanol, stained in fresh 1% Sudan Black B in 70% ethanol for 10 min at 60℃.
steps.searchable_summary: stain sections with Sudan Black B to detect lipids
